seo.title: Building Customer Support with Google ADK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Google ADK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) enables sophisticated multi-agent systems powered by Gemini models, handling RAG, CRM integration, and tool-calling for tickets and orders. However, multi-agent delegation, model inconsistencies, and session state issues can lead to circular dependencies, inaccurate responses, or PII leaks in high-stakes interactions. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify response accuracy, filter PII, ensure tone consistency, and trigger escalations before issues escalate.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Google ADK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Google ADK customer support architecture features a Router Agent that delegates to specialized sub-agents: RAG Agent for knowledge base retrieval, CRM Agent for order lookup via APIs, and Ticket Agent for creating support tickets. These agents collaborate in a session-based workflow, with Gemini models handling reasoning and tool calls. Long conversations maintain state across agents, but this introduces risks like circular delegation loops and format mismatches between tool responses.

The RAG Agent retrieves from vector stores using customer queries, while CRM integration pulls order history via APIs like Salesforce or Zendesk. Tool-calling enables actions like 'create_ticket' or 'lookup_order', orchestrated through ADK's agent execution graph. Session state tracks conversation history to maintain context, but corruption can occur in extended interactions.

Vex integrates as a before_agent_callback to inspect agent inputs/outputs, applying verifications for accuracy, PII, tone, policy, and escalation before execution proceeds.

risks.name: Multi-agent delegation creating circular dependencies
risks.description: Router Agent endlessly delegates between RAG and CRM agents without resolution
risks.mitigation: Vex detects delegation loops via session state analysis and forces termination/escalation

risks.name: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
risks.description: Different Gemini variants (1.5 Pro/Flash) produce varying tones or formats in ticket creation
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces tone consistency and response format validation across model switches

risks.name: Session state corruption in long conversations
risks.description: Conversation history truncation or mutation leads to context loss in multi-turn support flows
risks.mitigation: Vex validates session integrity and conversation continuity before agent execution

risks.name: Tool response format mismatches across agents
risks.description: CRM API returns vary from expected Pydantic schemas, breaking ticket creation workflows
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes tool outputs and validates against unified schemas before delegation

implementation.title: Basic ADK Customer Support Router Agent
implementation.description: Core router agent that delegates to RAG, CRM, and Ticket specialists using ADK's execution graph.

implementation.title: Session-Based Multi-Turn Conversation
implementation.description: Maintains conversation state across agent handoffs for complex support scenarios.
implementation.code:
from google_adk.sessions import Session

session = Session(agent=router)

# Multi-turn conversation
response1 = session.run('My order #12345 is late')
print(response1)  # 'Checking order status...'

response2 = session.run('Can you create a ticket?')
print(response2)  # 'Creating ticket for you...'

# Session state persists across turns
assert len(session.history) == 2

implementation.description: Structured CRM order lookup with schema validation for reliable data handling.
